npc.name: Gatekeeper Null
npc.dialogue: Halt, knight. Before you step deeper, prove you know what a variable truly is — a named box that holds value.

quests.title: Whispers at the Gate
quests.objective: Pass 3 trials on variables and naming.

challenges.prompt: What best describes a variable in programming?
options: A named storage location for data
challenges.explain: A variable is a named container that holds a value you can read and change.

challenges.prompt: Which is a valid Java variable name?
challenges.explain: Names start with a letter or underscore. totalCount is valid; class is a reserved keyword.
println prints the value stored in score, which is 10.

npc.name: Elder Syntax
npc.dialogue: These groves twist when types mix wrongly. int, double, boolean — speak their tongues and the path will straighten.

quests.title: Grove of Types
quests.objective: Master data type trials among the ancient trees.

challenges.prompt: Which type stores true or false?
challenges.explain: boolean holds logical values: true or false.
println outputs the double value 3.5 as stored.
Line 1 is missing a semicolon at the end.

npc.name: Operator Nymph
npc.dialogue: The stream flows only when operators align. +, -, *, / — one wrong symbol and the current reverses.

quests.title: Current of Operators
quests.objective: Solve operator riddles to calm the stream.

challenges.explain: The modulo operator % returns the remainder: 10 divided by 3 leaves 1.

challenges.prompt: What does the expression (5 > 3) evaluate to?
challenges.explain: The > operator compares values and returns a boolean. 5 is greater than 3, so true.

challenges.prompt: Which line will cause a problem?
challenges.explain: Dividing an int by zero throws an ArithmeticException at runtime.

npc.name: Bridge Warden
npc.dialogue: The bridge stones shift with prefix and postfix. Increment truly, or fall into the void below.

quests.title: Fix the Operator Bridge
quests.objective: Complete mixed trials to restore the crossing.

challenges.explain: Prefix ++x increments before use, so x becomes 6 and that value is printed.
Postfix x++ uses the old value first (5), then increments x to 6.

challenges.prompt: Arrange the lines to declare x as 5, then print it.
challenges.explain: You must declare the variable before you can print it.

npc.name: Dragon of Increment
npc.dialogue: I am the bug made flesh. Three riddles, knight — fail and the forest stays broken forever.

challenges.explain: Postfix a++ prints 10, then increments a to 11.

challenges.prompt: Find the line that breaks compilation.
challenges.explain: The println line is missing its semicolon.

challenges.prompt: Order the code to print 1 then 2.
challenges.explain: Declare t, print 1, increment to 2, print again.
